commit.message:
Fix false positives in exploit/scan modules (FW3.7.1)

- Add _discover_params(): crawl real page params before testing
- SQLi: use baseline comparison to filter pre-existing SQL-like text
- SQLi: time-based threshold now requires +4s vs baseline (not raw 2.5s)
- XSS: use unique session marker instead of generic payloads
- XSS: remove DOM sink false positives (innerHTML/eval exist everywhere)
- CMDi: use echo CMDI_CONFIRMED marker, remove generic 'ttl' indicator
- LFI: remove empty indicator payload (matched everything)
- LFI: use specific indicators (root:x:0:0:, HTTP_HOST=)
- vBulletin: require 'vbulletin' in response body (not just 302)
- upload_check: only report 200 with upload form content (not 403)
- sqli_scan: discover real params from page before injecting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Release WebGate FW3.7

New features:
- SSH backdoor via RCE CVE with Scope Agreement (typed 'I accept')
- TOR routing via bridge (obfs4/webtunnel/snowflake)
- SQLMap interactive dump dialog: pick DB -> table -> dump in GUI
- CVEExploitOfferDialog: auto-prompt when RCE CVE detected after scan
- ScopeAgreementDialog: typed 'I accept' confirmation before exploit
- SSHBackdoorDialog: configure SSH credentials + TOR bridge
- SQLMapDumpDialog: GUI-based DB/table selector with background dump

Improvements:
- Simplified reports: SQL injection Y/N + vulnerable param/payload
- XSStrike output simplified to injection Y/N + vulnerable URL
- Progress bar with gradient, % overlay, bright pulse edge animation
- Logo glitch animation (random interval glitch effect)
- Particle burst animation on scan start
- FW badge in logo canvas
- Scan-line border animation during active scan

Fixes:
- Removed COPY button (caused GUI freeze/lag on Wayland)
- Version strings updated to FW3.7
- LICENSE added (authorized use only)
- README.md fully rewritten with all features documented

commit.message:
fix: use setuptools.build_meta backend for broader compatibility

setuptools.backends.legacy:build was introduced in setuptools 69 and
breaks pip install -e . on environments with older versions. The
canonical setuptools.build_meta backend works across all modern versions.

Fixed in AGP 8.4.0. Also bump Compose compiler extension to match.

repository.name: flyPrint
repository.description: The direct print agent for the flyPush project

commit.message:
fix: draw horizon glow outside camera roll to close sky/grass gap

The horizon glow rect was drawn inside the camera-roll push/pop block.
When the camera tilts (e.g. during a lane swerve), the glow band rotates
around the vanishing point — on the far sides of the canvas it can shift
tens of pixels away from the actual horizY boundary, leaving the raw
sky-colour/grass-colour seam fully exposed on one side and doubly covered
on the other.

Moving the glow draw to before the camera-roll push keeps it axis-aligned
and ensures it uniformly covers the sky/grass boundary across the full
canvas width regardless of camera pan angle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove missing.md

fix: use temp file for ffmpeg thumbnails instead of signed URL

input parsing. Since we already have the video buffer in memory from
multer, write it to a temp file directly — simpler and more reliable.

commit.message:
perf(globe): Palantir-grade frontend performance optimization

Store batch(), event-driven compositor, rAF panel coalescing,
font loading consolidation, viewport zoom-gated factory dirtying,
intensity animation pool pre-allocation.

Key changes:
- ConsoleStore.batch() coalesces N set() calls into 1 notify per key
- Layer compositor idles at 0% CPU, renders only on dirty flags or active animations
- All panels use rAF coalescing to prevent DOM thrashing
- CSS @import font waterfalls replaced with single <link> in index.html
- Viewport pan no longer triggers 4 unnecessary factory rebuilds (zoom-gated)
- Intensity ink-in-water animation uses pre-allocated object pool (zero GC per frame)
- deselectEvent/syncRealtimeError batched, selectEvent double-fire eliminated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(examples): add four hardware test scripts

- wiring_map.exs: commission-time channel verification — toggles each
  EL2809 output individually and maps which EL1809 input responds.
  Found ch12 and ch15 open on real hardware.

- cycle_jitter.exs: self-clocking loopback jitter histogram — uses the
  output→input round-trip as a hardware-gated cycle timer. Reports
  p50/p95/p99/p99.9 with EXCELLENT/GOOD/FAIR/POOR verdict.

- rtd_stability.exs: EL3202 long-duration analysis using Welford online
  variance (no unbounded list), toggle-bit continuity check, outlier
  detection, and periodic statistical reports.

- watchdog_recovery.exs: stops domain cycling and measures EL2809
  watchdog behaviour. Revealed that EL2809 implements a silent SM
  watchdog: WDT_status expires and outputs go safe without an AL state
  change (stays OP). Also reads WDT_divider + WDT_SM to compute the
  actual configured timeout.

Also updates .iex.exs to current API (process_data_model,
encode_signal/decode_signal, mailbox_config, cycle_time_us).

Fix launcher redirect for multi-notebook builds

launch.html was hardcoded to redirect to index.html, which in multi-notebook
builds is a listing page (not a notebook viewer). Now the viewer path is
injected at build time via __VIEWER_PATH__ placeholder, and launcher JS paths
are computed relative to each notebook's directory depth.

during kind-reset, causing kubectl commands inside the Makefile
to fail. Clear it for the kind-reset step so Kind uses its default
kubeconfig management.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf(luau): strip exception-handling dead code (-18% output size)

Add Phase 3 to tree_shake_luau that recognizes and eliminates the
exception_last → const_none → is → not → if → error → end_if pattern.
In Luau there are no exceptions, so these blocks are always dead code.

The pass uses a sequential pattern matcher that:
- Nops simple dead kinds (check_exception, exception_stack_*)
- Recognizes the full 5-op exception check pattern
- Strips the entire if-block body (with nested if depth tracking)

Results: generator.py output 3481 → 2839 lines (-18.4%), same
correctness (933 platforms, 357 crystals, 11 anchors), same 23ms perf.

commit.message:
perf(ci): split integration-tests into parallel unit/rust/e2e jobs (#521)

* perf(ci): split integration-tests into parallel unit/rust/e2e jobs

Break the monolithic integration-tests job (616s) into 3 parallel jobs
that can run simultaneously:

- unit-tests: Vitest with coverage (~150s, needs detect-changes only)
- rust-coverage: cargo llvm-cov with --fail-under-lines 60 (~180s,
  needs build-images for custom postgres with pgmq)
- e2e-tests: KinD + Skaffold deploy + Playwright (~250s, unchanged
  dependencies)

Also adds a lightweight coverage-report job that downloads artifacts
from all three test jobs and assembles the unified coverage report.

Key improvements:
- Eliminates duplicate Rust test execution: skaffold test ran cargo
  llvm-cov (no threshold), then a separate step re-ran it with
  --fail-under-lines 60. Now it runs exactly once.
- Estimated critical path drops from ~616s to ~250s (e2e-tests).
- Vitest and Rust coverage start immediately after detect-changes
  (no build-images gate for Vitest, Rust waits for postgres image).

Tradeoffs:
- Web toolchain + WASM build duplicated in unit-tests and e2e-tests
  (~40s overhead per extra runner)
- Coverage report assembly requires a 4th lightweight job
- 3 separate logs to check on failure vs 1 monolith

Updates ci-gate, deploy-gitops, and deploy-reports to reference the
new job names.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(ci): fold coverage-report job into deploy-reports

Eliminate the standalone coverage-report job by moving its steps
(summarize-coverage, build-coverage-report, lcov install) into
deploy-reports which already downloads the same artifacts.

Removes one runner allocation (~10s), one web toolchain install
(~25s), and one artifact upload/download round-trip. Web toolchain
now only installs in unit-tests, e2e-tests, and deploy-reports.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): add unit-tests and rust-coverage to deploy-gitops needs

deploy-gitops previously only waited for e2e-tests, meaning a master
push with failing Rust or Vitest tests could still trigger a deploy.
Now waits for all three test jobs.

Also simplify if-guards on unit-tests and rust-coverage: their
dependencies (detect-changes, build-images) never skip, so always()
and the failure/cancelled checks are unnecessary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
